hyperedges.label: Dual Authentication Pattern (Staff Supabase + Client JWT)
Push Notification System (VAPID web + FCM mobile)

nodes.label: Next.js Breaking Changes Warning
nodes.rationale: Agents must read node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing code because Next.js 16 has breaking API/convention changes from prior versions the model may have trained on.

nodes.label: Next.js 16 + React 19 Stack
nodes.rationale: Uses App Router exclusively with no Pages Router; unstable_instant export required alongside Suspense for instant navigation performance.

nodes.label: Supabase Auth (Staff Authentication)
nodes.rationale: getUser() used server-side instead of getSession() for security; sessions managed via @supabase/ssr cookies.

nodes.label: src/lib/supabase-admin.ts (createAdminClient)
nodes.rationale: Uses service role key to bypass RLS; server-only, never imported in client components or Edge middleware.

nodes.label: middleware.ts (Edge Auth Middleware)
nodes.rationale: Edge-compatible only — bcryptjs and supabase-admin are excluded because they are not Edge-compatible.

nodes.label: src/lib/clientAuth.ts (Client JWT Auth)
nodes.rationale: Passwordless client portal auth via phone+name; uses jose JWTs with JWT_SECRET+"_client"; stored in localStorage as musa_client_token.

nodes.label: React Native + Expo SDK 52
nodes.rationale: Chosen because it shares the same JS ecosystem as the Next.js web app, enabling code reuse.

nodes.label: Expo Router (File-Based Routing)
nodes.rationale: Chosen for consistency with Next.js App Router file-based routing pattern.

nodes.label: Expo EAS (Cloud Builds)
nodes.rationale: Used to enable iOS builds from Windows without requiring a Mac.

nodes.label: NativeWind (Tailwind for React Native)
nodes.rationale: Provides the same Tailwind utility class system as the web for consistency.

nodes.label: AsyncStorage (Mobile Session Persistence)
nodes.rationale: Replaces browser localStorage for React Native; used for Supabase sessions and client JWT tokens.

nodes.label: PushSubscription DB Migration (FCM + platform column)
nodes.rationale: Extends PushSubscription to support both VAPID (web) and FCM (mobile) by adding platform, fcmToken, appVersion, deviceModel columns.

nodes.label: Google OAuth (React Native via expo-auth-session)
nodes.rationale: Uses expo-auth-session + expo-web-browser instead of @react-oauth/google which is web-only.

nodes.label: Cormorant Font (Editorial Typography)
nodes.rationale: Used exclusively for editorial moments, h1 headings, and welcome screens; font-normal only.

nodes.label: DM Sans Font (UI Base Typography)
nodes.rationale: Main UI font; hierarchy built via size/color only — font-semibold/bold/extrabold/black are prohibited.

nodes.label: DM Mono Font (Numeric Typography)
nodes.rationale: All numbers (prices, times, stats, counters) must use .font-mono-num class.

nodes.label: Heroicons (Exclusive Icon Library)
nodes.rationale: Only @heroicons/react/24/outline or solid permitted; material-symbols-outlined is prohibited.

nodes.label: Aesthetic Prohibitions (No Gradients, No MD3, No Generic SaaS)
nodes.rationale: MUSA uses solid clear colors; bg-gradient-to-*, generic SaaS aesthetics, and Material Design 3 tokens are all forbidden.
nodes.norm_label: aesthetic prohibitions (no gradients, no md3, no generic saas)

nodes.label: Skeleton Loading Pattern (animate-pulse)
nodes.rationale: Preferred over spinners when page structure is known.

nodes.label: Quiet Containers / Bento Grids Pattern
nodes.rationale: bg-surface/bg-surface-raised with border-outline-variant/30 and rounded-2xl/rounded-xl; aggressive shadows (shadow-md+) prohibited.

nodes.label: Letter U Arch Form (Upper Right Path)
nodes.norm_label: letter u arch form (upper right path)

nodes.label: Letter S Curve Form (Middle Right Path)
nodes.norm_label: letter s curve form (middle right path)

nodes.label: Letter A Stroke Form (Bottom Right Path)
nodes.norm_label: letter a stroke form (bottom right path)

nodes.label: Next.js Default Boilerplate SVG Icon Set
nodes.norm_label: next.js default boilerplate svg icon set

nodes.label: Browser Window Shape (rectangle with traffic-light dots)
nodes.norm_label: browser window shape (rectangle with traffic-light dots)

nodes.label: Flat Gray Icon Style (#666 fill, no stroke)
nodes.norm_label: flat gray icon style (#666 fill, no stroke)

links.notes: SVG comment states Generator: Adobe Illustrator 29.0.0, SVG Export Plug-In
180x180 viewBox with rx=40.79 rounded corners matches Apple Touch Icon specification for iOS home screen
Cream #f2e8d5 is a primary brand color used for the logotype foreground
Dark brown #34271e is a primary brand color used as the icon background
SVG contains a group of 4 path elements forming the Musa brand logotype
rect uses class st0 which maps to fill: #34271e (dark brown)
All wordmark paths use class st1 which maps to fill: #f2e8d5 (cream/off-white)

links.context: Four cream-colored paths form a stylized M monogram letterform, characteristic of the Musa brand
Favicon for browser tab; 180x180 viewBox scalable to 16x16/32x32/180x180 apple-touch sizes
SVG comment: Generator Adobe Illustrator 29.0.0 SVG Export Plug-In
rect class st0 maps to fill #34271e
All four path elements use class st1 mapping to fill #f2e8d5

links.source_location: path[3] bottom right stroke form representing A
path[0] large left-spanning path representing M
path[2] middle right curve form representing S
path[1] upper right arch form representing U
complex curved path data with flowing forms
dark fill #32261d suited for light backgrounds
identical path data flowing calligraphic forms
light fill #f2e8d7 suited for dark backgrounds
#32261d dark brown vs #f2e8d7 warm cream paired brand colors

links.context: The monogram letterform is rendered in cream ivory against the dark background
Background is a deep dark brown rounded square
The letterforms use high-contrast serif typography with elegant swashes and a teardrop terminal on the M
Favicon shows an M and T letterform monogram combined into a single ligature glyph
Icon uses a rounded-rectangle container consistent with iOS Android app icon conventions
The M initial stands for Musa; the combined MT monogram is the brand mark for the Musa app
Deep brown palette is associated with premium beauty and spa branding
High-contrast serif ligature with swashes is a common design language for luxury beauty salon and wellness brands
Musa is a beauty salon appointment platform; favicon aesthetic aligns with luxury wellness positioning

links.label: Next.js SVG uses black fill (#000) with wordmark spelling N-E-X-T and full text 'Next.js'
Vercel SVG is a white upward-pointing triangle (delta/chevron) on transparent background — classic Vercel logomark
